elements.sent-2411-001: We propose a universal matrix Capelli identity and explain how to derive Capelli identities for all quantum immanants in the Reflection Equation algebra and in the universal enveloping algebra $U(\mathbf{gl _{M|N )$.}}
elements.sent-2411-002: We present a universal matrix Capelli identity in the Reflection Equation algebra (Theorem 1).
elements.sent-2411-003: It is a far-reaching generalization of the classical Capelli identity in the Weil algebra (see [C]): \begin{equation}\label{class_cap} cdet(XD + K) = detX detD.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-004: Here $X = || x_i^j ||_{1\leq i,j \leq N}$ is a matrix of commuting variables, $D = || \frac{\partial {\partial x_j^i ||_{1\leq i,j \leq N}$ is a matrix of partial derivatives and $K = diag(N-1,N-2, \dots,1,0)$ is a diagonal numerical matrix.}}
elements.sent-2411-005: The symbol $cdet$ denotes the column determinant of a non-commutative matrix.
elements.sent-2411-006: There are known many generalizations of this identity.
elements.sent-2411-007: In particular, the paper \cite{OK1 proposes a version of the identity for quantum $\mu$-immanants associated with Young diagrams.}
elements.sent-2411-008: For example, the identity (\ref{class_cap ) corresponds to a single-column diagram with $N$ cells.}
elements.sent-2411-009: In \cite{OK2 a matrix version of this identity is proposed; the previous version is obtained by taking traces at all positions.}
elements.sent-2411-010: This version is called matrix, as well as all versions in which traces are not taken.
elements.sent-2411-011: Further generalizations of the Capelli identity are related to the Reflection Equation algebras, corresponding to the Hecke type $R$-matrices, they are called {\em quantum identities .}
elements.sent-2411-012: This name is motivated by the fact that if $R$ is the Drinfeld-Jimbo $R$-matrix, the corresponding Reflection Equation algebra is a deformation (quantization) of the universal enveloping algebra $U(\mathbf{gl _N)$.}
elements.sent-2411-013: Quantum matrix Capelli identities for the single-row and single-column diagrams were obtained in \cite{GPS1 (for any skew-invertible $R$-matrix).}
elements.sent-2411-014: In \cite{JLM this result was generalized to other Young diagrams but only for the Reflection Equation algebras associated with the Drinfeld-Jimbo R-matrices.}
elements.sent-2411-015: In this case quantum analogs of the immanants, introduced by A.
elements.sent-2411-016: Okounkov, also have been introduced in \cite{JLM .}
elements.sent-2411-017: Note that the universal matrix Capelli identity (\ref{Cap-quant ) holds in the Reflection Equation algebras corresponding to all skew-invertible Hecke $R$-matrices and it is not attached to any Young diagram.}
elements.sent-2411-018: All other forms of Capelli identity can be easily deduced from it.. {\bf 2.}
elements.sent-2411-019: For any matrix $A \in Mat_{N \times N ( \mathbb{C ) \otimes U$ with coefficients in an arbitrary vector space $U$ we use the notation: \begin{equation}A_i =  E \otimes  E \otimes \ldots \underset{i}{A} \ldots \otimes  E \in Mat_{N \times N}( C)^{\otimes k}\otimes U,\end{equation} where $\mathbb{E}$ is the identity matrix in the corresponding tensor component.}}
elements.formula-2411-002: \begin{equation}A_i =  E \otimes  E \otimes \ldots \underset{i}{A} \ldots \otimes  E \in Mat_{N \times N}( C)^{\otimes k}\otimes U,\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-020: Using this notation, one can write the relations in the universal enveloping algebra $U(\mathbf{gl _N)$ in the following form: \begin{equation}L_1 L_2 - L_2 L_1 = L_1 P_{12} - P_{12} L_1,\end{equation} where $L= ||L_i^j||_{1 \leq i,j \leq N}$ denotes the matrix composed of the algebra generators and $ P_{12}$ is a permutation matrix with the matrix elements $P_{kl ^{mn =\delta_k^n\, \delta_l^m$, $ 1\leq k,l,m,n\leq N$.}}}
elements.formula-2411-003: \begin{equation}L_1 L_2 - L_2 L_1 = L_1 P_{12} - P_{12} L_1,\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-021: Let us consider the Weil algebra $W_N$ generated by the elements of the matrices $X$ and $D$.
elements.sent-2411-022: These matrices satisfy the following relations: \begin{equation}X_1 X_2 = X_2 X_1, \quad D_1 D_2 = D_2 D_1,\quad D_1 X_2 = X_2 D_1 + P_{12}.\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-004: \begin{equation}X_1 X_2 = X_2 X_1, \quad D_1 D_2 = D_2 D_1,\quad D_1 X_2 = X_2 D_1 + P_{12}.\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-023: The algebra $U(\mathbf{gl _N)$ is embedded into the Weyl algebra via an embedding that maps the elements of the matrix $L$ to the corresponding elements of the matrix $XD$.}
elements.sent-2411-024: The universal matrix Capelli identity related to the algebra $U(\mathbf{gl _N)$ can be written in the following form: \begin{equation}L_{1} (L_{2} - j_2) \dots (L_{n} - j_n) = X_{1} \dots X_{n} D_{1} \dots D_{n},\quad \forall\,n\ge 1, \label{Cap-cl}\end{equation} where the elements $j_k = \sum\limits_{i = 1 ^{k-1 P_{i k}$ are the images of the Jucys-Murphy elements defined in the group algebra $\mathbb{C [S_n]$ of the symmetric group.}}}}
elements.formula-2411-005: \begin{equation}L_{1} (L_{2} - j_2) \dots (L_{n} - j_n) = X_{1} \dots X_{n} D_{1} \dots D_{n},\quad \forall\,n\ge 1, \label{Cap-cl}\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-025: As a straightforward consequence one can get the Capelli identity from \cite{OK1 : \begin{equation}Tr_{(1 \dots n)} \left(L_1 (L_2 - c(2)) \dots (L_n - c(n)) P_{ii}^{\lambda} \right) = Tr_{(1 \dots n)} \left(X_1 \dots X_n D_1 \dots D_n P_{ii}^{\lambda} \right). \label{imm}\end{equation} Here we use the notation $P_{ii ^{\lambda}$ for the primitive idempotents of the group algebra $\mathbb{C [S_n]$.}}}
elements.formula-2411-006: \begin{equation}Tr_{(1 \dots n)} \left(L_1 (L_2 - c(2)) \dots (L_n - c(n)) P_{ii}^{\lambda} \right) = Tr_{(1 \dots n)} \left(X_1 \dots X_n D_1 \dots D_n P_{ii}^{\lambda} \right). \label{imm}\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-026: Observe that the primitive idempotents are labeled by the standard Young tableaux $(\lambda, i)$, where the partition $\lambda\vdash n$ defines the form of the tableau and index $i$ numerates the tableaux of shape $\lambda$.
elements.sent-2411-027: The symbol $c(k)$ denotes the content of cell with number $k$ in the given standard tableau.
elements.sent-2411-028: We also use the notation $Tr_{(k)}$ for the trace in the $k$-th tensor component.
elements.sent-2411-029: Note that both parts of the equality (\ref{imm ) do not depend on the number $i$ of the primitive idempotent $P_{ii ^{\lambda}$.}}
elements.sent-2411-030: Besides, on omitting the trace in (\ref{imm ), we get the matrix Capelli identity from \cite{OK2 .. {\bf 3.}}}
elements.sent-2411-031: The Reflection Equation algebra $M(R)$ is a unital associative algebra generated by $N^2$ generators $m_i^j$ subject to \begin{equation}R_{12}M_1R_{12}M_1 = M_1R_{12}M_1R_{12},\quad M = \| m_i^j \|_{1\leq i,j \leq N}. \label{REA-M}\end{equation} Here $R \in Mat_{N \times N (\mathbb{C )^{\otimes 2}$ is the skew-invertible Hecke $R$-matrix, i.e. the following properties are satisfied: \begin{equation}R_1R_2R_1 = R_2R_1R_2, \qquad R^2 = 1 + (q-q^{-1}) R, \quad q \in C\backslash \{ 0, \pm 1\}\end{equation} and there exists a matrix $\Psi$ such that $Tr_{(2) (R_{12 \Psi_{23 ) = P_{13 = Tr_{(2) ( \Psi_{12 R_{23 )$.}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-032: Here and below we use notation $R_i = R_{ii+1}$ and assume that the parameter $q$ is generic, that is, $q^{k \neq 1,$ $\forall k \in \mathbb{Z _{>0}$.}}
elements.sent-2411-033: Using the linear change of the generators $\hat L = \frac{{\Bbb E - M {q - q^{-1 }$ we reduce the relation (\ref{REA-M ) to the form: \begin{equation}\hat L_1R_{1}\hat L_1R_{1} - R_{1}\hat L_{1}R_{1}\hat L_{1} = \hat L_{1} R_{1} - R_{1} \hat L_{1}. \label{mREA}\end{equation} In particular, for the Drinfeld-Jimbo $R$-matrix, the algebra defined by (\ref{mREA ) is a deformation of the universal enveloping algebra $U(\mathbf{gl _N)$.}}}}}}
elements.formula-2411-009: \begin{equation}\hat L_1R_{1}\hat L_1R_{1} - R_{1}\hat L_{1}R_{1}\hat L_{1} = \hat L_{1} R_{1} - R_{1} \hat L_{1}. \label{mREA}\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-034: Moreover, in the case when a Hecke $R$-matrix is a deformation of the super-permutation $P_{M|N}$, this algebra is a deformation of the algebra $U(\mathbf{gl _{(M|N) )$.}}
elements.sent-2411-035: Let us introduce a convenient notation: for an arbitrary $N\times N$ matrix $A$ we set \begin{equation}A_{\overline{k}} = R_{k-1} \dots R_{1} A_1 R_{1}^{-1} \dots R_{k-1}^{-1}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-036: Analogically to the classical case, the algebra $M(R)$ is embedded in the quantum Weyl algebra $W(R)$, which is the quotient-algebra of the free product of two Reflection Equation algebras $M(R)$ and $D(R^{-1 )$ (in the definition of the latter algebra, $R$ is replaced by $R^{-1}$).}
elements.sent-2411-037: The generating matrices of these two algebras are subject to the following permutation relations: \begin{equation}D_1 M_{\overline{2}} = R_1^{-1} + M_{\overline{2}}D_1 R_{1}^{-2}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-038: Thus, the Reflection Equation algebra is represented by quantum analogs of vector fields.
elements.sent-2411-039: Now we can formulate the main theorem.
elements.sent-2411-040: Here $J_1 = 1$, $J_k = R_{k-1 \dots R_2 R_{1 ^2 R_2 \dots R_{k-1}$, $k >1$ are the images of the Jucys-Murphy elements of the Hecke algebra with respect to the $R$-matrix representation.}}
elements.sent-2411-041: If $R$-matrix is a deformation of the permutation matrix, the universal matrix Capelli identity (\ref{Cap-cl ) in the algebra $U(\mathbf{gl _{N )$ is obtained from (\ref{Cap-quant ) by passing to the limit $q \rightarrow 1$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-042: Analogously, by taking any Hecke $R$-matrix, that deforms the super-permutation $P_{M|N}$, and by passing to the limit $q \rightarrow 1$, we obtain a similar identity in the superalgebra $U(\mathbf{gl _{(M|N) )$.. {\bf 4.}}}
elements.sent-2411-043: Note that for generic $q$ the Hecke algebra $\mathbb{H _n(q)$ of type $A_{n-1}$ is semisimple and is isomorphic to the group algebra of the symmetric group ${\Bbb C [S_n]$.}}
elements.sent-2411-044: In particular, the primitive idempotents $E_{ii ^{\lambda}$ of the algebra $\mathbb{H _n(q)$ are labeled by the standard Young tableaux.}}
elements.sent-2411-045: The set of Jucys-Murphy elements generates a maximal commutative subalgebra of the Hecke algebra and their action on the primitive idempotents is given by $J_k E_{ii ^{\lambda = q^{2c(k) E_{ii ^{\lambda}$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-046: Multiplying the equality (\ref{Cap-quant ) by the idempotent $E_{ii ^{\lambda}$ and using the last relation, we obtain the following identity: \begin{equation}\hat L_{\overline{1}} (\hat L_{\overline{2}} - q^{-c(2)}[c(2)]_{q}) \dots (\hat L_{\overline{n}} - q^{-c(n)}[c(n)]_{q}) E_{ii}^{\lambda} = q^{-2(c(1) + \dots + c(n))}M_{\overline{1}} \dots M_{\overline{n}} D_{\overline{n}} \dots D_{\overline{1}} E_{ii}^{\lambda}. \label{corCap}\end{equation} The quantum matrix Capelli identity from \cite{JLM was proved in this form.}}}
elements.formula-2411-012: \begin{equation}\hat L_{\overline{1}} (\hat L_{\overline{2}} - q^{-c(2)}[c(2)]_{q}) \dots (\hat L_{\overline{n}} - q^{-c(n)}[c(n)]_{q}) E_{ii}^{\lambda} = q^{-2(c(1) + \dots + c(n))}M_{\overline{1}} \dots M_{\overline{n}} D_{\overline{n}} \dots D_{\overline{1}} E_{ii}^{\lambda}. \label{corCap}\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-047: Also, in this paper quantum immanants were introduced in the Reflection Equation algebra associated with the Drinfeld-Jimbo $R$-matrix.
elements.sent-2411-048: In the general case quantum immanants can be obtained from the left-hand side of the equality (\ref{corCap ) by applying the $R$-traces $Tr_{R(12...n)}$ in all tensor components.}
